close_reason: StoryDAO (14 methods) + StoryRecordDAO (2 methods) wired with 43 passing tests
description:
TDD wiring of StoryDAO and StoryRecordDAO to Supabase. 16 methods total, ~48 tests.

Test files:
- frontend/src/server/data_access_objects/__tests__/StoryDAO.wiring.test.ts
- frontend/src/server/data_access_objects/__tests__/StoryRecordDAO.wiring.test.ts

CRITICAL IMPORT NOTES:
- DraftData is a LOCAL interface in StoryDAO.ts:21-28 (not from data_structures/) — import from '../StoryDAO'
- TruthCheck here is from @/server/data_structures/StoryRecord (interface with claim, verdict, evidence) — NOT from TruthCheck.ts
- DraftVersion, StoryMetrics, StoryRecord also from @/server/data_structures/StoryRecord
- ResumeData, JobData, QuestionData, SessionData are local interfaces in StoryDAO.ts

StoryDAO methods (14): findDraftById, findResumeById, findJobById, findQuestionById, findSessionById, findTruthChecksByDraftId, findDraftVersionsByDraftId, findMetricsBySessionId, insertStoryRecord, insertTruthChecks, insertDraftVersions, insertMetrics, getClaimsBySetId, saveDraft
- Tables: drafts, resumes, jobs, questions, sessions, truth_checks, draft_versions, story_metrics, story_records, claims
- Error: StoryError PERSISTENCE_FAILED from @/server/error_definitions/StoryErrors
- Note: draft_versions and story_metrics table names are inferred (no explicit comments in stubs)

StoryRecordDAO methods (2): findById, updateDraft
- Table: story_records
- Error: PersistenceError CLASS from @/server/error_definitions/PersistenceErrors (NOT the factory object from DomainErrors.ts). Class defaults retryable: true.
- Schema: DraftStoryRecordSchema uses mixed casing: id/status camelCase, truth_checks/draft_text/claims_used snake_case

Success criteria:

- npx vitest run StoryRecordDAO.wiring.test.ts — ~6 tests pass

description:
Manual debug findings (2026-03-03):

Symptoms observed in /writer flow:
1) First URL submission appears to fail in UI.
2) Reload + resubmit shows: "Channel initialization pipeline failed: A session is already active. Please finalize or end the current session before starting a new one."

Evidence:
- Supabase `sessions` row IS created for the submitted ZipRecruiter URL:
  id=5bd75eb4-d999-4423-a81b-6146eb90db1d, state=initialized, created_at=2026-03-03T16:50:42.663+00:00
- That row has `updated_at = null`.
- Session view contract currently requires `updatedAt: string` (frontend/src/server/data_structures/SessionView.ts).
- /api/sessions/[sessionId] validates response via SessionViewSchema and returns 500 when updatedAt is null, so navigation to /session/[id] fails after successful ingestion.
- Because the session remains in `initialized`, subsequent /api/ingestion/url attempts fail via InitializeSessionDAO.getActiveSession() with SESSION_ALREADY_ACTIVE, surfaced by ChannelIngestionPipelineAdapter.

Likely fix options:
- Set `updated_at` at insert time in InitializeSessionDAO.persist (and include in return).
- Or relax SessionViewSchema/mapper to tolerate null updatedAt for legacy rows.
- Add regression test covering URL ingestion -> /session/[id] load with a fresh initialized session row.

Additional risk:
- Active session uniqueness check is global (`sessions.state = initialized`) and not user-scoped; may block across users.
title: Fix URL-first session handoff: newly created session has null updated_at and breaks /session page

description:
Problem
A final verification pass is needed to prove the pipeline is operational across source modes and progression paths after implementation.

Implementation Scope
- Run targeted automated tests for recall progression and source-aware routes.
- Run a manual smoke checklist for both source modes:
  - A/A/O updates from spoken content
  - move-on voice command behavior
  - manual next button behavior
  - final transition to Review
- Capture any residual gaps and create follow-up issues if needed.

Verification Checklist (minimum)
1. `answer_session` flow: content persists, progress updates, move-on works, manual next works.
2. `session` flow: content persists, progress updates from correct record source, move-on/manual next both work.
3. No 409/500 boundary regressions in normal progression path.

Risks
- Environment-only issues (auth tokens, realtime permissions) may not reproduce in unit tests.
- Manual checks can miss race conditions without deliberate repetition.

Acceptance Criteria
1. Test run summary attached to issue notes or closing reason.
2. Manual checklist completed for both source types.
3. Any discovered residual defects are filed as linked beads issues.
title: Run targeted verification for recall progression pipeline

close_reason: Implemented migration to align confirm_story RPC signature/response with DAO contract; added migration validation test.
description:
Manual repro: in voice-assisted session, Select a Story -> Confirm triggers CONFIRMATION_FAILED with message: RPC failed: Could not find the function public.confirm_story(p_question_id, p_story_id) in the schema cache.

Findings:
- DAO calls supabase.rpc('confirm_story', { p_question_id, p_story_id }).
- Migration defines confirm_story(p_story_id text, p_claim_ids text[], p_new_status text default 'CONFIRMED'), so PostgREST cannot resolve requested arg names.
- DAO expects return shape { confirmed_story_id, excluded_count } but function currently returns { story_id, claims_updated, new_status }.
- Two newer migration files exist but are empty (0 bytes), suggesting missing follow-up migration.

Likely fix: add/replace SQL function to match API contract for path 313 (confirm selected story + exclude others by question), apply migration, and add migration-level verification test for arg names + return keys.
title: Fix confirm_story RPC signature mismatch causing story confirm failure

description: State enum casing is inconsistent: 'initialized' (lowercase) vs DRAFT/ACTIVE (uppercase). The Session TypeScript enum omits 'initialized'. Normalize all states to UPPER_CASE, add INITIALIZED to the TS enum, update DB CHECK constraints. Low risk, high clarity gain.
title: Normalize session state enums to consistent UPPER_CASE

close_reason: Implemented strict source-aware recall progress loading across loader, route, and RecallScreen with regression tests
description:
Problem
Recall progress (Anchors/Actions/Outcomes) is loaded using only sessionId, but story record storage is split by source. For `answer_session` rows, data is keyed by `voice_session_id`; for legacy `session` rows, data is keyed by `session_id`. Current progress route resolves via voice-session alias only, so legacy session-source flows can show neutral progress even when content exists.

Implementation Scope
- Update recall progress loading contract to include source (`answer_session` | `session`) alongside sessionId.
- Thread source from RecallScreen -> loader -> `/api/recall/progress` query params.
- In `/api/recall/progress`, resolve story records by source-aware lookup, not voice-only alias.
- Keep backward compatibility behavior explicit (e.g., default source handling) and document it.

Files/Areas Likely Touched
- `frontend/src/components/RecallScreen.tsx`
- `frontend/src/data_loaders/RecallProgressLoader.ts`
- `frontend/src/app/api/recall/progress/route.ts`
- `frontend/src/server/data_access_objects/SessionDAO.ts` (source-aware read helper if needed)

Risks
- Breaking older callers that still pass only sessionId.
- Returning wrong source data if source fallback is ambiguous.

Acceptance Criteria
1. For `sessionSource=answer_session`, progress reflects `voice_session_id` story record content/responses.
2. For `sessionSource=session`, progress reflects `session_id` story record content/responses.
3. RecallScreen always requests progress with both sessionId and sessionSource.
4. No regressions for existing answer_session flow.
5. Tests cover both sources explicitly.
title: Make recall progress loading source-aware across session types

description: Extracted from HAR trace (session 7d14f077, 2026-03-05). Two compounding bugs: (1) advance_question and update_working_answer resolve different story records via different DAO paths, causing currentIndex regression from 1→0 after advance. (2) LLM goes silent after session.update — needs response.create to auto-speak. TLA+ model in artifacts/tlaplus/voice-advance-regression/. Buggy model: BackendConsistency + NoLLMSilenceAfterAdvance VIOLATED. Fixed model: all 10 properties PASS (219 states, 90 distinct).
title: TLA+ model: voice-advance-regression (HAR-proven backend record divergence + LLM silence)

close_reason: Completed
description:
## Summary
File upload-to-generation behavior regressed after merge reconciliation. The attach UI exists, but non-audio file content is no longer carried through to the `/api/generate` request, and attachment metadata/rendering from the previous pipeline is missing.

## User-Visible Regression
- Expected: uploaded text/image files are included in generation context and reflected in message metadata/UI.
- Actual: send flow ignores attachment payloads for generation path; attachment pipeline artifacts from prior implementation are absent.

## Prior Working Pattern (reference)
Canonical end-to-end implementation exists in commit `3675209`:
- Client-side payload prep and send wiring:
  - `frontend/src/lib/file-content.ts` (validation + payload transformation)
  - `frontend/src/app/page.tsx` (snapshot files, call `prepareFilesContent`, pass attachments to API)
  - `frontend/src/lib/api.ts` (3rd argument for attachments)
- Message metadata + rendering:
  - `frontend/src/lib/types.ts` (`attachments` on `Message`)
  - `frontend/src/components/chat/MessageBubble.tsx` (attachment list UI)
- Server-side ingestion:
  - `frontend/src/app/api/generate/route.ts` (attachment limits + user content assembly)
- Tests:
  - `frontend/__tests__/integration/file-send-flow.test.tsx`
  - `frontend/__tests__/api/generate-route.test.ts`

## Current Divergent Path
- Current `handleSendMessage` path does not prepare or pass attachment payloads.
- `frontend/src/lib/file-content.ts` is absent.
- `frontend/src/lib/api.ts` only sends `message` and `history`.
- `generate` route currently accepts no `attachments` field.

## Suspected Cause
Merge resolution dropped file-pipeline hunks (introduced in `3675209`) while retaining newer voice and generate-route changes, resulting in partial UI-only upload behavior.

## Scope
- Reintroduce attachment pipeline without regressing newer generate-route behavior (including current prompt/BAML constraints where applicable).
- Restore invariant: uploaded files attached at send time are represented in message state and API payload.
- Maintain existing audio transcription upload flow as-is.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Sending a message with uploaded supported files includes attachment payloads in `/api/generate` request.
2. User message stores attachment metadata and displays attached files in conversation UI.
3. Server route validates attachment limits and correctly folds text/image attachment content into model input.
4. Unsupported file types fail gracefully with user-visible error and do not crash send flow.
5. Coverage restored for end-to-end file-send flow and generate-route attachment handling.

## Verification Notes
- Manual check with at least one text file and one image file.
- Confirm no regressions to voice transcription upload (`/api/upload` + `/api/transcribe`) paths.
title: Regression: file upload content no longer reaches generation pipeline

title: Consolidate /api/session and /api/sessions route namespaces

close_reason: Wired move-on intents to guarded auto-advance with dedupe, in-flight guard, refresh-aware gating, and telemetry
description:
Problem
Voice trigger phrases like “next question” / “move on” are detected, but current behavior only opens stop controls and does not advance progression automatically. This creates mismatch with coaching instructions and user expectation.

Implementation Scope
- Route move-on intent through progression flow when guard conditions allow advancement.
- If required slots are still incomplete, keep current guidance behavior and do not advance.
- Ensure transcript events for control intent do not incorrectly persist as answer content.
- Preserve telemetry semantics (intent detected, advanced vs blocked reason).

Files/Areas Likely Touched
- `frontend/src/components/RecallScreen.tsx`
- Optional telemetry event payload updates if needed.

Risks
- False positives from intent regex advancing unexpectedly.
- Double-advance if same event is processed twice.

Acceptance Criteria
1. Saying “next question”/“move on” advances when progression guards pass.
2. If guards fail, UI stays on current question with explicit missing-slot guidance.
3. No duplicate advancement on repeated/deduped transcript events.
4. Regression tests validate both advance and blocked paths.
title: Wire voice move-on intents to guarded question advancement

description: Extracted behavioral model from InitializeSessionDAO.ts, GetSessionHandler.ts, SessionView.ts, ChannelIngestionPipelineAdapter.ts. V2: extended with fixed transitions (4 fixes modeled). 6 invariants — 5 VIOLATED in buggy mode, all HOLD in fixed mode. TLA+ parameterized by mode constant. Spec: specs/orchestration/url-ingestion-error-space-model.md (v2). TLA+: artifacts/tlaplus/url-ingestion-error-space-model/UrlIngestionErrorSpace.tla (manual, rich) + UrlIngestionErrorSpaceModel.tla (auto-generated, verified). Related bug: silmari-writer-1fp.
title: TLA+ model: URL ingestion error space

close_reason: Fixed and committed: answer_session INIT no longer uses session UUID as questionId; ORIENT only runs with real question context
description: After fixing /api/sessions/undefined, session workflow for source=answer_session enters ORIENT stage and calls GET /api/story/orient-context?questionId=<session-uuid>. In SessionWorkflowShell, OrientStoryModule is invoked with questionId={session.id}. For answer_session this id is not a question id, causing 'Question not found: <sessionId>' errors. Need to either provide a real questionId in SessionView or avoid ORIENT stage for answer_session INIT.
notes:
Debug findings (2026-03-02): SessionWorkflowShell passes questionId={session.id} into OrientStoryModule. OrientStoryModule calls /api/story/orient-context?questionId=<that value>. For source=answer_session, GetSessionHandler returns only id/state/source and no questionId, so session UUID is used as questionId and ConfirmStoryDAO.findQuestionById misses with Question not found. Evidence: frontend/src/modules/session/SessionWorkflowShell.tsx line 81, frontend/src/data_loaders/loadOrientStoryData.ts line 25, frontend/src/app/api/story/orient-context/route.ts lines 28-31, frontend/src/server/request_handlers/GetSessionHandler.ts lines 11-17.
Reconfirmed on 2026-03-02 debug retry: /writer still routes to StartSessionRouteAdapter -> StartVoiceSessionModule -> createSession -> /session/:id. SessionWorkflowShell currently passes questionId={session.id} (frontend/src/modules/session/SessionWorkflowShell.tsx:81) into OrientStoryModule. That loader calls /api/story/orient-context?questionId=<session-id> (frontend/src/data_loaders/loadOrientStoryData.ts:25), and route throws DATA_NOT_FOUND Question not found for that UUID (frontend/src/app/api/story/orient-context/route.ts:28-31). GetSessionHandler returns SessionView without questionId (frontend/src/server/request_handlers/GetSessionHandler.ts:11-17; frontend/src/server/data_structures/SessionView.ts). answer_sessions schema has no question_id (supabase/migrations/20260302000000_initial_schema.sql:71-77). story_records table has question_id but createStoryRecord insert omits it (frontend/src/server/data_access_objects/SessionDAO.ts:141-143).
Implemented fix (2026-03-02): added optional questionId to SessionView/AnswerStoryRecord, mapped story_records.question_id in SessionDAO, and GetSessionHandler now returns questionId from story record for answer_session. Session stage mapping is now source-aware: INIT for answer_session without questionId maps to RECALL_REVIEW instead of ORIENT. SessionWorkflowShell now passes OrientStoryModule questionId={session.questionId} only when present and no longer uses session.id. Added unit coverage in modules/session tests for skip-orient and questionId prop wiring. Quality gates could not run in this environment because node/npm/npx are not installed.
title: Fix orient context query using sessionId as questionId

close_reason: Resolved recall progression wiring defects across source lookup, slot math, and voice progression behavior
description:
Debug findings (2026-03-05): voice-loop has multiple inoperative links in session-source (`session`) flow.

Evidence:
1) Anchors/Actions/Outcomes panel can stay neutral forever for legacy session-source flows.
   - `frontend/src/app/api/recall/progress/route.ts` calls `SessionDAO.findStoryRecordBySessionId(sessionId)`.
   - `SessionDAO.findStoryRecordBySessionId` is an alias to `findStoryRecordByVoiceSessionId` (voice_session_id lookup only).
   - Legacy session-source writes persist to `story_records.session_id` via `upsertPrepStoryRecordWorkingAnswer`, so progress route misses these rows and returns zeros.

2) Slot completeness logic is effectively disabled once any transcript exists.
   - In `computeRecallProgress`, anchors/actions/outcomes use `Math.max(1, countPatternHits(...))`.
   - Therefore each metric is >=1 for any non-empty corpus, making `incompleteSlots` always empty in non-empty cases.

3) Voice commands do not advance question progression.
   - `RecallScreen.detectMoveOnIntent` catches "next question" / "move on".
   - Event handler calls `presentStopState('move_on_intent')` and returns.
   - It never calls `handleNextQuestion` / `advanceSessionQuestion`, so progression requires manual button click despite coach copy saying app listens for these trigger phrases.

Expected:
- Session-source progress route should resolve correct story record source (or accept source in API).
- Incomplete slot computation should allow zero values when signals are missing.
- Move-on intents should optionally trigger progression (or copy should be changed to match behavior).

Suggested tests:
- Add `recall/progress` test covering source=session story_records via session_id.
- Add unit tests for `computeRecallProgress` with non-empty text lacking action/outcome patterns.
- Add RecallScreen test verifying move-on intent progression behavior (auto-advance or explicit non-advance contract).
title: Fix recall progression wiring for session-source flows

close_reason: Closed
description:
TDD wiring of OnboardingDao and SessionMetricsDAO to Supabase. 7 methods total, ~21 tests.

Test files:
- frontend/src/server/data_access_objects/__tests__/OnboardingDao.wiring.test.ts
- frontend/src/server/data_access_objects/__tests__/SessionMetricsDAO.wiring.test.ts

OnboardingDao methods (5): findByUserAndStep, updateOnboardingStep, createOnboarding, insertAnalyticsEvent, findAnalyticsEvent
- Tables: onboarding, analytics_events
- Error: BackendErrors from @/server/error_definitions/BackendErrors
  - CRITICAL: Import from BackendErrors.ts, NOT from SmsErrors.ts which exports a shadowing BackendErrors aggregate of SMS-specific errors
  - CRUD ops: BackendErrors.PersistenceFailed
  - insertAnalyticsEvent: BackendErrors.AnalyticsPersistenceFailed

SessionMetricsDAO methods (2): getSessionWithEvents, upsertSessionMetrics
- Tables: sessions + events (join), session_metrics
- Error: MetricsPersistenceError — STANDALONE FUNCTION EXPORT from @/server/error_definitions/MetricsErrors (no MetricsErrors namespace exists). Import as: import { MetricsPersistenceError } from '@/server/error_definitions/MetricsErrors'
- SPECIAL: getSessionWithEvents queries across sessions + events tables. Two implementation options:
  Option A (preferred): supabase.from('sessions').select('*, events(*)').eq('id', sessionId).single()

  Mock must accommodate chosen pattern.

close_reason: Removed slot count floor and added regressions to preserve true missing-slot zeros
description:
Problem
Current recall progress computation forces each metric to be at least 1 when corpus is non-empty. This can make missing slots invisible and causes `incompleteSlots` to appear complete too early.

Implementation Scope
- Remove floor-to-1 behavior from anchors/actions/outcomes computation.
- Compute counts directly from pattern hits and preserve zero values when a slot signal is absent.
- Re-derive `incompleteSlots` from true counts.
- Keep neutral behavior for empty corpus intact.

Files/Areas Likely Touched
- `frontend/src/app/api/recall/progress/route.ts`

Risks
- Threshold changes may alter UX copy frequency for “missing slots”.
- Existing tests asserting >0 for any non-empty corpus will need updates.

Acceptance Criteria
1. Non-empty corpus can still produce zero for missing dimensions.
2. `incompleteSlots` includes dimensions with zero signal.
3. Empty corpus remains `{anchors:0, actions:0, outcomes:0}` with all slots incomplete.
4. Route tests cover mixed-content examples (anchor-only, action-only, outcome-only).
title: Fix recall slot completeness math to preserve true missing slots

description: Current 28-day and all-time baseline has n=0 across session_metrics, analytics_events, and primary_kpi_events, blocking decision-usable KPI deltas. Wire emission/persistence for leading KPI and primary KPI events, validate writes in integration tests, and produce first non-zero baseline snapshot.
title: Bootstrap leading KPI telemetry coverage (session_metrics, analytics_events, primary_kpi_events)

description: Three init services (SessionInitService, SessionInitializationService, InitializeSessionService) and three DAOs (SessionDAO, InitializeSessionDAO, SessionInitDAO) have overlapping concerns. Audit usage, consolidate to one service + one DAO, deprecate or remove unused code. Moderate effort.
title: Consolidate session init services and DAOs

Implementation Scope
- Keep existing manual button controls and labels intact.
- Ensure voice-intent changes do not remove or degrade manual click path.

Risks

- Final-step transition could regress (stuck at last question).

Acceptance Criteria

2. Manual Finish to Review still triggers review transition at terminal question.
3. Manual controls remain visible/usable in stop-control state.
4. Tests explicitly cover manual path independent of voice intent path.

description:

BLOCKING DECISION: migrate Path 294 contract to embedded shape (breaking) or keep legacy path until deprecation.

3. ENHANCE (Section 1): stageMapper key fix must ship as atomic changeset with all literal state contracts (startSessionFromUrl.ts:12, startSessionDefaultQuestions.ts:24, startSessionFromUpload.ts:48, ChannelIngestionPipelineAdapter.ts:13, StartVoiceSessionModule.tsx:38) + DB migration.

Additional warnings (enhance): snapshot test, route backward compat, system-backfill sentinel, StoryStatus→StoryRecordStatus scope.

close_reason: Unified manual and voice progression through shared advancement flow in RecallScreen
description:
Problem
Progression logic is currently split across local state updates, API calls, and separate voice/manual branches, increasing drift risk and inconsistent behavior.

Implementation Scope
- Consolidate progression into a single shared progression action/function.
- Have both button-click and voice-intent invoke the same progression primitive.
- Ensure shared path executes source-aware `/api/session/voice-turns` advance action and reconciles local/server state consistently.
- Standardize error handling and fallback (local optimistic update vs server authoritative update).

Files/Areas Likely Touched
- `frontend/src/components/RecallScreen.tsx`
- Possibly `frontend/src/api_contracts/sessionVoiceTurns.ts` only if contract adjustments are needed.

Risks
- Introducing race conditions between local and server progression updates.
- Breaking one source mode while fixing another.

Acceptance Criteria
1. Voice and manual progression call the same internal progression path.
2. Question index, active question, and persisted questionProgress stay consistent.
3. Source-aware server advancement remains correct for both `answer_session` and `session`.
4. Error path leaves UI in a recoverable state (no dead-end).
title: Unify voice and manual progression through one advancement path

close_reason: Completed
description:
## Summary
After merge reconciliation, the assistant message **Edit** entrypoint is no longer connected to the voice-edit workflow. Clicking `Edit` in the assistant button ribbon opens the legacy text modal only, and there is no path from that entrypoint into realtime `voice_edit` mode.

## User-Visible Regression
- Expected: from message-level editing flow, voice edit mode is available/usable.
- Actual: `Edit` opens `EditMessageModal` (text-only), and voice mode is not reachable from that flow.

## Prior Working Pattern (reference)
- Voice-edit capability was introduced and verified in:
  - `7df1b55` (Voice Edit API + hook + panel)
  - `28c58b7` (page integration with voice controls)
  - `1800214` (stabilization of voice integration)
- Existing voice architecture is still present and should be reused:
  - `frontend/src/hooks/useVoiceEdit.ts`
  - `frontend/src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts`
  - `frontend/src/components/chat/VoiceEditPanel.tsx`

## Current Divergent Path
- Message-level edit button still routes to legacy modal flow:
  - `frontend/src/components/chat/ButtonRibbon.tsx` (`handleEditClick` → `setIsEditModalOpen(true)`)
  - `frontend/src/components/chat/EditMessageModal.tsx` (text-only UI)
- This bypasses `useVoiceEdit.startEditing()` and `VOICE_MODES.VOICE_EDIT`.

## Suspected Cause
Merge retained the legacy `ButtonRibbon/EditMessageModal` edit path without integrating the voice-edit entrypoint, so the voice implementation exists but is effectively disconnected for message-level edit action.

## Scope
- Reconnect message-level edit UX to voice edit mode (either mode picker or direct voice path with explicit fallback).
- Preserve existing non-voice editing behavior where intended.
- Ensure store updates and undo behavior still use the existing voice edit history flow.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Clicking assistant `Edit` provides a working path into voice edit mode (not text-only dead-end).
2. Voice edit path invokes `useVoiceEdit.startEditing()` and establishes realtime session with `VOICE_MODES.VOICE_EDIT`.
3. Edited content is applied through existing store update path and supports undo (`useConversationStore.popEdit`).
4. Automated coverage exists for message-level edit → voice mode path (unit/integration test).
5. Existing text edit behavior (if retained) remains functional and explicitly test-covered.

## Verification Notes
- Validate UI behavior manually on a live conversation with at least one assistant message.
- Confirm no regression to read-aloud controls while entering/exiting voice edit mode.
title: Regression: message-level Edit no longer exposes voice edit mode

description: Observed during silmari-writer-1lq verification: voice commands can move the coach to a different question while the Recall header still shows stale 'Question N of N'. Evidence from production network capture: /api/recall/progress request for sessionSource=session returned questionProgress currentIndex=0 activeQuestionId=q-default-1 despite voice navigation having moved around. In code, RecallScreen renders the counter from questionProgress.currentIndex but resolves question text by activeQuestionId fallback (getQuestionByProgress); if these fields diverge, label/text drift. Also, backward voice intents are not explicitly synchronized via session/voice-turns progression API. Add regression test for mismatched currentIndex vs activeQuestionId and enforce a single source of truth for rendered index + persisted progression updates during voice command navigation.
notes:
2026-03-05 debug evidence: user captured consecutive GET /api/session/voice-turns?sessionId=21fc30c6-87b2-44b8-89b0-df6ebffe7656&sessionSource=session responses with identical questionProgress {currentIndex:0,total:4,activeQuestionId:q-default-1}. This confirms persisted question_progress is not advancing for session-source flow despite perceived voice navigation.
2026-03-05 runtime trace: on spoken 'next question' in session-source flow, observed event chain: recall_move_on_intent -> GET /api/recall/progress returns anchors/actions/outcomes=0 with all incompleteSlots -> recall_move_on_blocked -> recall_stop_state_presented (twice). No observed advance event in this sequence, consistent with blocked progression path and missing /advance_question execution.
title: Fix recall question counter drift in voice navigation

description: Pushing to Dolt remote... fails in this repo. On bd 0.56.1 it failed with 'no store available'. After upgrading to 0.57.0 via doctor, failure is explicit: remote 'origin' not found in Dolt repo. Root cause appears to be missing Dolt remote configuration for database beads_silmari-writer on server 127.0.0.1:3307. Additional side effect: doctor started a second local Dolt server at 127.0.0.1:14274 with DB name 'dolt', causing metadata/DB name mismatch and potential issue visibility confusion.
notes:
Debug findings (2026-03-02):
- Reproduced both failures: bd dolt push/pull => fatal: remote 'origin' not found.
- Active server is systemd service dolt.service on 127.0.0.1:3307, WorkingDirectory=/home/maceo/.dolt-data/beads.
- beads_silmari-writer exists and is queryable; dolt_remotes table is empty.
- .beads/dolt-monitor.pid is stale, so 'bd dolt status' reports not running even though systemd server is active.
- MOTD script (/etc/update-motd.d/99-services-status) only reports service health; does not configure remotes.
- Cross-check against TROUBLESHOOTING#database-issues: local shadow DB exists at .beads/dolt with DB 'dolt' and 0 issues; active systemd server DB beads_silmari-writer has 24 issues. This matches documented stale shadow DB pattern, but active push failure remains missing Dolt remote on beads_silmari-writer.
Possible fix (2026-03-04):
- Symptom: bd dolt push/pull failed with 'no common ancestor' even after origin remote existed.
- Root cause hypothesis: active Beads DB pointed at wrong database ('dolt' instead of project DB), plus stale divergent remote-tracking refs (remotes/origin/main) from an old history in both server DB and remote path.

Recovery steps that worked:
1) Ensure Beads targets the project DB:
   bd dolt set database beads_silmari-writer
2) Verify active server + DB:
   bd dolt show
   # should show host 127.0.0.1, port 3307, database beads_silmari-writer
3) Rebuild filesystem remote from source-of-truth DB:
   source: /home/maceo/.dolt-data/beads/beads_silmari-writer
   target: /home/maceo/.dolt-remotes/silmari-writer
   (backup old target first)
4) Reset remote metadata on source DB and force upstream rewrite:
   cd /home/maceo/.dolt-data/beads/beads_silmari-writer
   dolt remote remove origin
   dolt remote add origin file:///home/maceo/.dolt-remotes/silmari-writer
   dolt push -f -u origin main
   dolt fetch origin

Validation:
- bd dolt push => Push complete
- bd dolt pull => Pull complete

Important note:
- Running 'dolt init' in ~/.dolt-remotes/silmari-writer can create/reset independent history and trigger this mismatch.
- Multiple git repos in one parent directory are not the issue by themselves; per-remote Dolt history divergence is.
title: Fix beads dolt push/pull remote configuration

description: answer_sessions.user_id is NOT NULL but sessions.user_id is nullable (added via migration). Backfill existing rows, add NOT NULL constraint. Ensures consistent auth/scoping across both session types.
title: Make sessions.user_id NOT NULL for auth correctness

close_reason: Fixed and pushed: SessionPage now resolves params safely and avoids /api/sessions/undefined requests
description: Writer flow creates a valid UUID session via POST /api/sessions, navigates to /session/{uuid}, then SessionPage calls getSession(params.sessionId) where params is not resolved in client runtime. This issues GET /api/sessions/undefined and returns 400 INVALID_REQUEST: Session ID must be a valid UUID. Reproduced locally with Playwright: POST /api/sessions => 200 {sessionId:<uuid>}, followed by GET /api/sessions/undefined (twice).
title: Fix /session route passing undefined sessionId to API

Local working tree has an unstaged RecordButton.tsx update that adds label and ariaLabel and resolves this mismatch. Need to commit and push that file (or stop passing those props).
title: Fix RecordButton prop typing mismatch in Recall flow
